name: Creative Inventory Action
desc: Player sets the contents of a slot in creative mode. Used to spawn items or modify inventory contents directly.

fields.name: slotId
fields.desc: Slot index (-1 for cursor, 0-44 for inventory, 100-104 for armor)

notes: Only processed by the server if the player is in creative mode (gamemode=1). Ignored silently in survival. Can set ANY slot including armor (100-104) and offhand slots. No known hacked client modules directly target this packet — it's used by vanilla creative mode mechanics. Gugustus has a CreativeGive command that constructs C10 via addToSendQueue.

implementation.overview: C10PacketCreativeInventoryAction allows creative-mode players to set inventory slot contents. Slot ID ranges: -1 (cursor), 0-35 (main inv), 36-44 (hotbar), 100-103 (boots/legs/chest/helmet), 104 (offhand in 1.9+). The server ONLY accepts this in creative mode. Modules like CreativeGive construct C10 with custom ItemStacks containing NBT data for spawning items. No major module in Rise 6.0, RektSky B5, Sigma 4.11, or Jigsaw 0.26 targets this packet — it's handled entirely through vanilla GUI mechanics.

modules.name: CreativeGive / Item Spawn
modules.purpose: Spawn any item in creative mode by sending C10 with a custom ItemStack. Used for giving items to other players or spawning items with custom NBT (enchantments, lore, display names).
modules.how_it_works: The module finds an empty slot, creates an ItemStack with the desired item/NBT, and sends C10 to place it. The player then picks the item up from the slot. This is how creative servers allow item spawning — it's a legitimate creative-mode mechanic that modules automate.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayServer.processCreativeInventoryAction(C10PacketCreativeInventoryAction) — only processes if player is in creative mode. Sets inventory slot contents directly.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: No anti-cheat concern — this packet only works in creative mode where item spawning is legitimate. Some survival servers ignore this packet silently.

implementation.general_hooks: Client: GuiContainerCreative sends C10 when dragging items from creative tabs. Modules rarely target it. The packet flows through NetworkManager.sendPacket() and fires event hooks in all major clients, but no module in Rise 6.0, RektSky B5, Sigma 4.11, Jigsaw 0.26, Memeware 7.3, or Aqua 13 specifically intercepts C10.
implementation.client_variations: Gugustus: CreativeGive command sends C10 with custom ItemStacks. No other major client has a module specifically targeting C10.
